features.summary: Prologis reported strong Q4 2024 results with record quarterly leasing of 60 million square feet and core FFO of $1.50 per share including promotes. Full-year core FFO growth of 8.4% placed the company in the 86th percentile of all REITs. Average occupancy was 95.8% for the quarter and 96.3% for the year, with net effective rent change of 66% quarterly and 69% annually. The company successfully recycled $2.1 billion in dispositions and reinvested $2.3 billion in acquisitions at a positive 170 basis point IRR spread.
features.management_outlook: Management expects market conditions to improve with vacancy topping out and rents inflecting later in 2025. They forecast 20% improvement in net absorption for 2025 with completions declining 35% below 2024 levels. Development starts are guided at $2.25-2.75 billion with data center projects expected at 200-400 megawatts. The company sees 10 gigawatts of data center development potential over the next 10 years and expects to reach 1 gigawatt of solar generation by year-end 2025.
features.risks: Key risks include potential occupancy decline in the next 1-2 quarters before rebuilding, with average occupancy guided at 94.5%-95.5%. Bad debt is expected to increase to 20-30 basis points from historical levels below 20 basis points due to known tenant concerns. Market rent growth remains uncertain with potential for flat, down, or modest positive growth in 2025. The Los Angeles wildfire impact is still being assessed, and broader economic uncertainties around trade policy and immigration could affect operations.
features.key_drivers: Post-election leasing acceleration has driven improved customer decision-making and a 17% increase in pipeline activity January-to-January. The company maintains a significant 30% lease mark-to-market representing $1.4 billion of incremental NOI potential. Replacement costs are 50% higher than in-place rents, providing long-term rent growth runway. The data center business generated $112 million in value creation fees from the Elk Grove transaction, demonstrating the platform's monetization capabilities and comprehensive development expertise.
